additionalTime: 10 minutes patty seasoning rest and sauce rest
author: Robert Carr
description: A fast one-serving burger for a 2-ounce A5 Wagyu blended beef patty package, with a toasted store-bought potato or brioche bun, quick soy onions, light burger sauce, pickles, lettuce, and a careful skillet sear that keeps the rich beef front and center.
equipment: Cast-iron skillet or stainless steel skillet

groceryIngredients.display: A5 Wagyu blended beef burger patties
notes: Central Market package shown is 2 patties totaling 2 ounces / 57 grams
groceryIngredients.original: A5 Wagyu blended beef burger patties

groceryIngredients.item: butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce

ingredients: 2 A5 Wagyu blended beef burger patties, 2 ounces / 57 grams total, kept cold until cooking
1 small potato bun or brioche slider bun, ideally 2 1/2 to 3 inches wide
1 thin slice American cheese, torn or folded into 2 patty-size pieces
1/4 small yellow onion, with 2 tablespoons sliced paper-thin for the pan onions
1 small butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce leaf, patted dry
Small pinch garlic powder, about 1/16 teaspoon
1/8 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or about 1/16 teaspoon Morton kosher salt, divided
1/8 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground, divided
Small pinch MSG, about 1/16 teaspoon, optional
instructions: Put the 2 A5 Wagyu blended beef patties on a paper towel-lined plate and pat the tops and sides very dry.
Mix 1/8 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/8 teaspoon black pepper, and the small pinch MSG if using in a small bowl.
Season both sides of the 2 patties with almost all of the salt, pepper, and MSG mixture, saving only a tiny pinch of the mixture for the onions.
Refrigerate the seasoned patties uncovered for 10 minutes while you make the sauce and prepare the toppings.

Make the burger sauce by stirring together 1 tablespoon mayonnaise, 1/2 teaspoon ketchup, 1/2 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 1/4 teaspoon pickle brine, 1/4 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, and the small pinch garlic powder.
Refrigerate the sauce for 10 minutes while the patties rest so the mustard, pickle brine, and Worcestershire season the mayonnaise.
Slice 2 tablespoons yellow onion paper-thin, then separate the slices with your fingers so they cook quickly.
Pat 1 butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce leaf dry and set out 3 to 4 dill pickle chips so the burger can be assembled immediately after the patties finish.
Melt 1 teaspoon unsalted butter in a cast-iron or stainless steel skillet over medium heat.
Add the 2 tablespoons sliced yellow onion and the reserved tiny pinch of the salt, pepper, and MSG mixture.
Cook the onions for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring often, until softened and lightly browned at the edges.
Stir 1/2 teaspoon low-sodium soy sauce, the remaining 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine, 1/8 teaspoon granulated sugar, and 1 teaspoon water into the onions.
Cook for 30 to 60 seconds, stirring constantly, until the liquid is gone and the onions look glossy, then transfer the onions to a small plate.
Wipe the skillet clean if any onion sugar stuck to the pan, then return the skillet to medium heat.
Spread the remaining 1 teaspoon unsalted butter over the cut sides of the small potato bun or brioche slider bun.
Toast the bun cut-side down for 1 to 2 minutes, until the cut sides are golden and crisp at the edges, then transfer the bun to a plate.
Increase the skillet to medium-high heat for 1 minute. The pan should be hot enough to brown the patties quickly but not so hot that the rendered Wagyu fat smokes hard.
Pat the cold seasoned patties dry again if surface moisture appears.
Place the 2 patties in the hot dry skillet and cook without pressing for 60 to 90 seconds, until the bottoms are deeply browned.
Flip the patties with a thin metal spatula.
Place half of the American cheese slice on each patty immediately after flipping.
Cook for 60 to 90 seconds more, until the cheese melts and the centers of the ground beef patties reach 160 degrees F when checked from the side with an instant-read thermometer.
If the cheese needs help melting, turn off the heat and cover the skillet for 10 to 15 seconds, keeping the patties in the pan only long enough to soften the cheese.
Spread about 2 teaspoons burger sauce on the toasted bottom bun.
Add the dry butter lettuce or red leaf lettuce leaf and 3 to 4 dill pickle chips to the sauced bottom bun.
Stack the 2 cheese-topped Wagyu patties on the pickles.
Spoon all of the glossy soy onions over the patties.
Spread the remaining burger sauce on the toasted top bun.
Close the burger and serve immediately while the bun is crisp and the patties are hot.
notes: This recipe is built for the package in the photo: 2 small patties totaling 2 ounces / 57 grams. Use the whole package for 1 small, rich burger.

A slider-size potato bun or brioche bun fits the photographed patties better than a full-size burger bun. If you only have a standard bun, use the soft center and trim the edges after toasting so the bun does not overwhelm the beef.

Buying the bun is the better choice for this fast version. Making buns from scratch would push the meal well past the 30-minute target and would not improve the burger as much as careful cooking does.

The main flavor moves are a dry surface, light exterior seasoning, a short cold rest, a hot dry pan, quick soy-pickle onions, melted American cheese, pickles, and a restrained sauce that does not bury the Wagyu.
Do not smash these patties unless they are much thicker than the ones in the photographed package. With very small Wagyu patties, pressing hard can squeeze out too much rendered fat.
Do not add oil to the skillet before searing the patties. Wagyu-style ground beef renders quickly, and extra oil can make the burger greasy instead of rich.
For food safety, cook ground beef patties to 160 degrees F. These small patties cook quickly, so start checking as soon as the second side has browned.
If your Wagyu patties are larger than 2 ounces total, season the exterior with about 0.75% kosher salt by meat weight and extend the cook time until the centers reach 160 degrees F.
Keep the sauce light. The burger should taste like rich beef first, then toast, onions, cheese, pickles, and sauce.
Serve with fries, chips, or a small salad. The burger itself is small but very rich.
title: A5 Wagyu Burger
